changes.value:
Add builtin view definitions to swissarmyhammer-kanban and integrate with KanbanContext, following the exact pattern of fields.

## Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/views/board.yaml` — builtin board view definition
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` — add BUILTIN_VIEWS static, builtin_view_definitions()
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — add views field, load+merge in open(), views() accessor
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` — add swissarmyhammer-views dependency

## board.yaml
```yaml
id: 01JMVIEW0000000000BOARD0
name: Board
icon: kanban
kind: board
entity_type: task
commands: []
```

## defaults.rs additions
- `static BUILTIN_VIEWS: Dir = include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/builtin/views");`
- `pub fn builtin_view_definitions() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)>` — same pattern as builtin_field_definitions()

## context.rs additions
- Add `views: Option<Arc<ViewsContext>>` field to KanbanContext
- In `open()`: create `.kanban/views/` dir, load builtins + local overrides, build ViewsContext
- Add `pub fn views(&self) -> Option<&ViewsContext>` accessor

## Checklist
- [ ] Add swissarmyhammer-views to kanban Cargo.toml
- [ ] Create builtin/views/board.yaml
- [ ] Add BUILTIN_VIEWS and builtin_view_definitions() to defaults.rs
- [ ] Integrate views loading into KanbanContext::open()
- [ ] Add views() accessor
- [ ] Tests: builtin views load, merge with local overrides, views() returns Some after open()
- [ ] cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban passes

#phase2-views

#phase2-views
\ No newline at end of file
+Add builtin view definitions to swissarmyhammer-kanban and integrate with KanbanContext.

Builtins are **seed data** — written to `.kanban/views/` on first open only (don't overwrite user changes). After seeding, all views are loaded from disk via ViewsContext.

## Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/views/board.yaml` — builtin board view definition
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` — add BUILTIN_VIEWS static, builtin_view_definitions()
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — add views field, seed+load in open(), views() accessor
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` — add swissarmyhammer-views dependency

## board.yaml
```yaml
id: 01JMVIEW0000000000BOARD0
name: Board
icon: kanban
kind: board
entity_type: task
card_fields:
  - title
  - tags
  - assignees
  - progress
commands:
  - board.lane
  - board.newCard
  - board.collapseAll
  - board.expandAll
```

## defaults.rs additions
- `static BUILTIN_VIEWS: Dir = include_dir!("$CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR/builtin/views");`
- `pub fn builtin_view_definitions() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)>`

## context.rs changes
- Add `views: Option<Arc<ViewsContext>>` field to KanbanContext
- In `open()`: create `.kanban/views/` dir, seed builtins (write-if-absent), build ViewsContext from disk
- Add `pub fn views(&self) -> Option<&ViewsContext>` accessor

## Seeding logic
For each builtin view YAML: if `.kanban/views/{name}.yaml` doesn't exist, write it. If it does exist, skip (user may have modified it). Then load all views from `.kanban/views/` directory.

## Checklist
- [ ] Add swissarmyhammer-views to kanban Cargo.toml
- [ ] Create builtin/views/board.yaml
- [ ] Add BUILTIN_VIEWS and builtin_view_definitions() to defaults.rs
- [ ] Implement seeding logic in context.rs open()
- [ ] Add views() accessor
- [ ] Tests: builtin views seed on first open, don't overwrite on second open, views() returns Some
- [ ] cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban passes

#phase2-views
\ No newline at end of file

 Add builtin view definitions to swissarmyhammer-kanban and integrate with KanbanContext.

Builtins are **seed data** — written to `.kanban/views/` on first open only (don't overwrite user changes). After seeding, all views are loaded from disk via ViewsContext.

- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs` — add views field, seed+load in open(), views() accessor
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` — add swissarmyhammer-views dependency

## board.yaml
```yaml

  - assignees

  - board.newCard
  - board.collapseAll
  - board.expandAll
```

- In `open()`: create `.kanban/views/` dir, seed builtins (write-if-absent), build ViewsContext from disk
- Add `pub fn views(&self) -> Option<&ViewsContext>` accessor

For each builtin view YAML: if `.kanban/views/{name}.yaml` doesn't exist, write it. If it does exist, skip (user may have modified it). Then load all views from `.kanban/views/` directory.
